description_long: This is a long, rectangular row house complex set on a grassy plot with a paved driveway and walkways. The building features a series of connected units, each with its own entrance, and is constructed primarily from red brick with a dark, shingled gable roof. Windows are arranged in symmetrical rows along both the front and rear facades, providing ample natural light to each unit. The front of the house has a small paved area leading to individual doors, while the rear includes a driveway and parking spaces. The structure is surrounded by a low, brown retaining wall, and the landscaping is minimal, with grass covering the majority of the lot. The overall design is functional and straightforward, typical of suburban or urban residential developments.
description: A long, red-brick row house with a dark gable roof, symmetrical windows, and individual entrances, set on a grassy lot with paved walkways and a driveway.
description_view_0: This view shows the front facade with individual entrances, symmetrical windows, and the main walkway leading up to the house.
description_view_1: This angle highlights the side of the house, showing the end unit and the continuation of the walkway to the street.
description_view_2: The rear view reveals the driveway, parking spaces, and back entrances to the units, as well as the full length of the building.
description_view_3: This perspective emphasizes the rear and side of the house, including the driveway, parking, and the grassy lot surrounding the structure.
attribute_entryway_design: Individual front doors for each unit, accessed by a paved walkway, with minimal overhangs and no prominent porch or columns.
